meta_title: For Students: idea validation to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to idea validation and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Idea Validation to Solve Debugging Roadblocks
content.seo_description: Master idea validation to overcome debugging fatigue. A student-focused guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to build robust, career-defining software.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't a lack of skill; it's a lack of validation. By validating your core logic before writing complex code, you reduce the surface area for errors. Use Antigravity to architect your logic paths and Claude Code to automate the heavy lifting, ensuring every line serves a verified purpose.
content.intro:
The screen glows, the cursor blinks, and the console screams a wall of red text. You feel that familiar tightening in your chest—the 'debugging deadlock' where the more you fix, the more you break. Every student hits this wall, but the elite builders have learned that most bugs aren't technical failures; they are architectural flaws born from unvalidated ideas.

Stop fighting the syntax and start engineering the solution. By shifting your focus toward rigorous idea validation, you transform your workflow from a reactive scramble into a precise, systematic build. This guide bridges the gap between your raw ambition and professional-grade software, turning your debugging nightmares into clear, actionable development milestones.

steps.title: Define the Minimal Viable Logic
steps.description: Before writing code, map out the core loop of your feature. This prevents 'feature creep' that leads to unmanageable debugging scenarios.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this feature request and generate a flow-chart of the logic dependencies, identifying potential edge cases where state could become inconsistent.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'map out the core logic flow for user authentication and state persistence'

steps.title: Automate Unit Test Generation
steps.description: Create a safety net by generating tests before implementation. If the test fails, you know exactly where the logic breaks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Based on the logic map, write a set of Jest unit tests that cover the primary success path and three critical edge cases.

steps.title: Simulate Data Flow
steps.description: Verify your data structures. Most bugs are actually just mismatched data shapes between your frontend and backend.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a mock JSON schema for this API response and validate it against the TypeScript interface defined in my project.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'validate data schema consistency across api and client models'

steps.title: Iterative Refactoring
steps.description: Don't fix the whole file. Use Claude Code to isolate the specific function causing the stack trace and refactor it in isolation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this function stack trace. Rewrite the function to be pure, immutable, and easier to test.

steps.title: Final Integration Audit
steps.description: Run a holistic audit of your codebase to ensure your changes didn't create regressions in unrelated modules.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a dependency analysis on the recent changes and identify any potential side effects on the user dashboard module.

pro_tips: Always treat your error messages as data, not as a personal failure; use them as direct inputs for your LLM prompts.
If you find yourself debugging for more than 30 minutes, stop and rewrite the function from scratch using an LLM-assisted clean slate.
Prioritize 'Type-Safety' early; most 'bugs' are just bugs that the compiler would have caught if you had defined your types correctly.
Use Claude Code to document your 'Why' in the comments as you fix issues; this creates a self-documenting history of your learning.
Adopt the 'TDD-AI' loop: Write the test, ask the AI to write the code to pass it, then audit the code for security flaws.
content.next_steps: After mastering this validation workflow, start contributing to open-source repositories to test your architecture against real-world, large-scale codebases.
content.conversion_block: Stop guessing and start building with the precision of a senior engineer. You have the tools, the strategy, and the roadmap to ship your first production-ready application. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
